projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The 'side_yard' camera is currently experiencing severe technical errors, making it impossible to verify activity in the driveway or street.
The 'side_yard' camera is experiencing a significant stream failure with heavy visual artifacts, which prevents reliable monitoring of the driveway and street.

There is a highly regular, repeating 30-minute cycle (White Noise, Leak Detected, Lights Off) that persists throughout the morning (05:44, 06:14), even though the family is awake and Miller is observed outside. This suggests a scheduled, non-human routine (e.g., a faulty timer or system test) that needs investigation, as it is occurring independently of the household's activity.

Energy synthesis (May 15): 1. **Overnight Baseline:** The baseline load fluctuates between 1.01 kWh and 1.72 kWh, averaging approximately 1.5 kWh/hr during the 12 AM–5 AM window. This is likely driven by "always-on" loads: the refrigerator, Wi-Fi routers, security systems, and standby power from electronics.

2. **Evening Spi

There is a highly regular, repeating 30-minute cycle (04:14, 04:44, 05:14) involving white noise activation and a detected leak (0.0 gal/min). This suggests an automated, scheduled routine that may be running regardless of household activity and should be checked to ensure the leak detection is not a false positive or a necessary system check.
Miller is active on the street at 5:28 AM.

There is a highly regular, repeating 30-minute cycle (03:14, 03:44, 04:14) involving white noise and minor leak detection, strongly suggesting an automated sleep routine. This routine continues until 04:14 AM, immediately preceding the drop in presence from 14 to 13 devices at 04:21 AM, indicating one person or device has left the main area or home after the sleep cycle was established.
Steve slept only 2.31h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

There is a highly regular, repeating pattern every 30 minutes (02:14, 02:44, 03:14) involving the activation of white noise and minor leak detections. This suggests a scheduled 'Sleep Optimization' or 'Good Night' routine is running repeatedly, which may be unnecessary or could be adjusted to save energy.

At 02:03 AM, the presence signal increased from 13 to 14 mobile devices, indicating a single device became active or connected during the established deep sleep cycle. This suggests a brief, late-night activity or device wake-up that deviates from the expected quiet state.
